title: Fast fashion drove Bangladesh - now its troubled economy ...What’s happening in Bangladesh’s garment industry?Overcoming Challenges in Bangladesh's Textile & Garment Sector
snippet: Sep 6, 2024 ·Bangladeshis the beating heart of the global fast fashion business. The clothes its factories export stock the shelves at H&M, Gap and Zara. Over three decades, this has transformed the country ...Mar 26, 2025 ·Bangladeshis well-known for its low-cost production of textiles and clothing, with much of the country’s recent development resting on rapid growth in the garment industry. But behind this progress is an unsustainable reliance on low pay and poor working conditions, particularly for women.Is Bangladesh preparing for LDC graduation in 2026?Since Bangladesh is the largest exporter by value among the LDCs, Bangladesh is projected to experience the most substantial reduction of $ 5.37 billion in value among all the graduating countries (Figure 3). Despite the growth of its RMG sector, one of the most pressing issues isthe country’s impending LDC graduation in 2026.Does Bangladesh have a ready-made garment industry?In particular,the ready-made garment (RMG) sector occupies an outsized position in Bangladesh’s economy– and by extension, in the national imagination. Widely regarded as the engine of development, the rise of the RMG industry was meteoric. In 1983-84, apparel accounted for less than 4% of total Bangladeshi exports.What is Bangladesh's role in the textile and clothing industry?The textile and clothing industry is one themain sources of Bangladesh’s recent economic growth and development. In particular, the ready-made garment (RMG) sector occupies an outsized position in Bangladesh’s economy – and by extension, in the national imagination.Where is a worker sorting undergarments in Bangladesh?[1/2]A worker sorts undergarments at the packing section of a garment factory inAshulia, on the outskirts of Dhaka, Bangladesh, April 19, 2025. REUTERS/Fatima Tuj Johora Purchase Licensing RightsDespite the growth of its RMG sector, one of the most pressing issues is the country’s impending LDC graduation in2026.
